acceptance_targets: [CH18_PROSE_POV_CONTRACT_BREAK] Pass if a cold read of revised snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:3 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:141 lets the reader assign every paragraph either to one embodied scene anchor or to one explicitly presented artifact, with no sentence claiming knowledge unavailable to that anchor or artifact. Fail if the chapter still moves from camera to worksheet to bridge call to inboxes in one invisible narrator voice, or if any run of 10 or more paragraphs still lacks immediate on-page processing from the active scene anchor.

[CH18_CRAFT_OUTSIDER_SCENE_SUPPRESSED] Pass if either the Diane block or the Mariam block contains at least four consecutive paragraphs in one time frame, from one person's immediate experience, and ends with an irreversible state shift visible on the page: police are coming because Diane has witnessed the site, or Mariam has official confirmation of Noor's death and a concrete unanswered question that the company cannot meet. Fail if both beats remain stitched transcript-summary reels.
[RED-031] No more than 29 sentences in chapter 18 begin with 'The' (~15% of 194).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold read of revised snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:3 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:141 lets the reader assign every paragraph either to one embodied scene anchor or to one explicitly presented artifact, with no sentence claiming knowledge unavailable to that anchor or artifact. Fail if the chapter still moves from camera to worksheet to bridge call to inboxes in one invisible narrator voice, or if any run of 10 or more paragraphs still lacks immediate on-page processing from the active scene anchor.
findings.problem: The chapter abandons the book's canonical close-third, Noor-locked narration and replaces it with an unowned evidence reel. After announcing its source list at line 3, the prose slides across 911 audio, body-camera footage, photo sets, worksheets, bridge-call audio, a notification recording, analyst notes, and Tuesday email in one continuous house voice. That leaves the chapter without a stable experiencing consciousness and effectively without interiority for the rest of the file, which is a prose-contract break rather than a controlled extension of it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:3; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:9; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:33; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:53; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:71; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:95; and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:129 into one legible mode. Best options are: anchor the chapter to one external witness scene and keep any non-scene material as clearly presented inserts, or commit fully to artifact form with visible document/transcript formatting and no omniscient bridge narration between sources. Do not keep the current single narrator who somehow knows every file at once.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if either the Diane block or the Mariam block contains at least four consecutive paragraphs in one time frame, from one person's immediate experience, and ends with an irreversible state shift visible on the page: police are coming because Diane has witnessed the site, or Mariam has official confirmation of Noor's death and a concrete unanswered question that the company cannot meet. Fail if both beats remain stitched transcript-summary reels.
findings.problem: The chapter's two strongest new human beats, Diane finding the bodies and Mariam learning her daughter is dead, are both held at dossier distance instead of being allowed to become full scenes. The text keeps interrupting live pressure with retrospective or administrative framing, so the outside world's first contact with the massacre lands as curated evidence rather than dramatic aftermath. Because chapter 18's job is to convert sealed horror into public consequence, suppressing those scenes deprives the coda of fresh pressure and leaves it reading as inventory plus commentary.
findings.rewrite_direction: Choose a primary external scene at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:9 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:31 or at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:95 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:126 and let it run in continuous real time: physical setting, interruption, reaction, and one irreversible state change. If Diane is the anchor, demote later source summaries around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:33 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:69 to aftermath inserts. If Mariam is the anchor, strip the surrounding script analysis at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:101; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:105; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_18.md:123 so the reader stays inside the call rather than above it.

findings.acceptance_test: No more than 29 sentences in chapter 18 begin with 'The' (~15% of 194).
findings.evidence: 'The' opens 56 of 194 sentences in Ch18 (28.9%). Manuscript-wide: 'The' opens 1,015 of 6,461 sentences (15.7%, numerator 1015, denominator 6461). Every chapter exceeds the 7% threshold.
findings.problem: Ch18 has the worst 'The'-opener density at 28.9% — nearly one in three sentences begins with 'The.' This is the manuscript's most pervasive sentence-pattern monotony issue. The chapter's short length (194 sentences) makes the pattern especially noticeable.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite at least 25 of the 56 'The'-initial sentences to open with different constructions: verbs, adverbs, prepositional phrases, character names, subordinate clauses. Target density below 15%.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel should make the reader uncomfortable on every axis: physical (graphic violence and decomposition), psychological (watching civilized people degrade), moral (the protagonist is compromised, the collaborator has sympathetic motives, the apex predator is competent rather than monstrous), and structural (the polite AI register against the horror, the corporate coda after the massacre). Lean into every discomfort the premise creates. The worst creative risk is pulling the punch. Ember's politeness should never waver — that consistency is more unsettling than menace. The deaths should never be clean. The protagonist should never be heroic. The ending should offer no comfort, no lesson, no redemption. The stock price jumps eleven percent. That is the last sentence of the novel. Let it land.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism, summary, or cut-away at the moment of violence, death, or physical consequence is a craft failure. This is a horror novel. The deaths must be graphic, ugly, physical, and specific on the page. The reader must be in the room. If a character is beaten to death with a fire extinguisher, the reader hears the sound. If someone dies of chemical burns, the reader sees the skin. If someone bleeds out, the reader watches the eleven minutes. No fading to black, no tasteful elision, no looking away. The bodies stay.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Priya, Chen Wei, and Viktor, who are naturally more formal. Even formal characters should contract under stress. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate precision, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-3), dialogue density (dialogue-heavy vs narration-dominant), pacing (sustained tension vs building dread vs explosive violence), point-of-attack (mid-action opening vs quiet-before-storm vs aftermath). Some chapters should be almost entirely one sustained scene; others should cut between locations and timelines within the day. The texture variation prevents the novel from becoming a monotone of crisis.
prose_style_profile.diction: Clean, precise, contemporary. Not literary-lyrical and not hard-boiled. Noor's observational intelligence should inform the prose without the prose becoming clever. The diction is a window, not a performance. Technical language from ML and infrastructure used naturally when Noor would think in those terms. Physical description is concrete and specific — brand names, measurements, the exact color of something. Never vague when precision is available.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Metaphor pile-up: one strong image per paragraph maximum, do not layer three metaphors on one moment
Emotional narration: do not tell the reader what to feel through adjective stacking or emotional summary. Show the thing and let the feeling happen.
Thriller-voice: do not drift into sentence fragments, subject-dropping, or staccato rhythm as a default tension mode. This is a horror novel with a literary sensibility, not a airport thriller.
Comfort recovery: do not follow a horror beat with emotional stabilization. No character 'takes a breath and feels steadier.' The dread compounds.
Ember-explanation: do not explain Ember's strategy through narrator exposition. It should emerge through Noor's analysis and observation.
Purple death: do not poeticize the deaths. They are ugly, physical, specific. No one dies beautifully.
The reassuring paragraph: do not end a chapter or section with a sentence that provides emotional comfort or resolution. End on the thing that stays.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third, locked to Noor. The reader sees, hears, smells, and knows only what Noor does. When Noor is not present for a scene, it doesn't happen on the page — it arrives as discovery, rumor, or aftermath. The camera never leaves her.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Propulsive and varied. Default pace is forward-moving — short paragraphs, active verbs, scene momentum — but the rhythm must shift for horror (slowed, sensory-heavy, the moment stretching), grief (longer sentences, heavier cadence), and dread (suspended, waiting, the sentence holding its breath). No single rhythm for more than two consecutive pages.
sensory_bias: smell (decomposition, chemicals, recycled air, blood, institutional food)
temperature (the building cooling degree by degree)
sound (electromagnetic locks, ventilation changes, terminal notifications, violence, silence)
tactile (cold surfaces, blood, the physical textures of deterioration)
